checks.finding: The `## Architecture` section (design.md:43-90) substantively covers all three required elements: component boundaries via the 4-subgraph mermaid diagram plus the Components table (design.md:94-104, e.g. `tests/guard-dispatch-fallback.tests.sh`, `sdd-hook-guard.{py,js,ps1,sh}`); integration with existing components (arrows showing GDF/GNC driving the live guard binaries, TESTYML_HC human-copying to TESTYML_LIVE, HUMAN applying the candidate); and technology choices per component (Bash/Python/Node/PowerShell/GitHub-Actions-YAML/Markdown in the Components table, appropriate for this non-UI Feature Type). However, the diagram's own `RC` node at design.md:82 reads `RC["required-checks (needs: updated to preserve BL-001 semantics)"]` -- stating `needs:` is UPDATED -- which directly contradicts design.md's own repeated, explicit statements elsewhere in the same document that `needs:` stays byte-identical: design.md:353-358 ("`required-checks`' `needs:` list is UNCHANGED in membership"), design.md:426-428 (Design Decisions OQ-5: "preserving `required-checks`' `needs:` membership exactly"), design.md:529 (Constraint Compliance: "membership UNCHANGED"), and infra-spec.md:32 ("UNCHANGED membership"). This also contradicts the binding spec-level decision that `required-checks`' `needs:` is byte-unchanged. Concrete failure mode: a reader who treats the Architecture diagram as the canonical integration summary (the diagram is the section's leading artifact) could conclude the design intends to edit `needs:`, creating ambiguity about the exact required-checks contract Stream D's staged candidate must produce -- the same surface AC-017's self-check verifies. Not precheck-owned (precheck only checks hash/status). Severity calibrated to Major rather than the check's nominal Critical default because the surrounding majority of the document (5+ locations) states the correct intent clearly and this is a single mislabeled diagram node, not a missing element.

The Architecture section's mermaid diagram (design.md:45-90) and Components table (design.md:94-104) describe a directed acyclic graph: new test suites (GDF, GNC) depend on live guard binaries (SHGSH/SHGPY/SHGPS/SHGJS) one-way (invoke, never written back to, per the read-only discipline stated at design.md:106-111 and Security Boundaries B1); PWSHSTUB/CWDCORPUS feed into GDF/GNC one-way; ADR0010/LOOPINV feed into WFSCEN (Stream C) one-way; TESTYML_HC feeds into TESTYML_LIVE and RC one-way, with HUMAN as the sole writer of TESTYML_LIVE. No node has an edge back to an ancestor -- e.g. `sdd-hook-guard.sh` never depends on `tests/guard-dispatch-fallback.tests.sh`, and `required-checks` never depends back on `test.yml`'s human-copy staging path. No cycle exists in either subgraph or across subgraphs.

The `## Data Plan` section (design.md:181-195) explicitly addresses all three required sub-fields: `Data Entities:` states "none new for Streams A, B, D" and names Stream C's one deferred new schema (the closed `greenfield`|`brownfield` scenario-schema field, design.md:183-188); `Existing Data Affected:` explicitly states "none" with rationale (design.md:190-193); `Migration Strategy:` explicitly states "none. No schema changes to any existing artifact" (design.md:195). This matches the check's requirement that a no-data-change feature explicitly state so for each sub-field rather than omitting the section.

The `## API / Contract Plan` section (design.md:197-360) is non-empty and thoroughly documents each new file's internal contract for this CLI/CI-only feature (appropriate substitute for HTTP endpoints given the declared non-UI Feature Type). However, its Stream D subsection contains a direct, load-bearing contradiction of the design's own binding constraint. design.md:339-346 states: "the restructuring introduces ONE new job, `deterministic-tests-boundary`... that runs a single, trivial, always-green marker step immediately BEFORE the existing `test` job's own step list begins" -- this is reiterated as a deliberate decision at design.md:426-427 (Design Decisions OQ-5: "adds one trivial boundary-marker job"). This directly contradicts: (1) requirements.md:231-244 REQ-004, which states "the job graph is unchanged" and the change is scoped "WITHOUT splitting the job"; (2) requirements.md:435-439 AC-016, which states "the job count and job names are unchanged" and specifies only "a documented, currently-empty comment placeholder marking where a future LLM-invoking eval lane would be added" (i.e. a YAML comment, not an actual executing job); and (3) the orchestrator-supplied binding spec-level decision that the job graph is byte-unchanged. The contradiction is corroborated by its absence elsewhere: design.md's own Architecture mermaid (SD subgraph, design.md:79-87) shows no `deterministic-tests-boundary` node, and infra-spec.md's Deployment Topology diagram (infra-spec.md:14-37) shows `TESTJOB --> RC` directly with no intervening marker job -- confirming this is an internal contradiction, not a consistently-applied design choice. Concrete failure mode: an implementer following the API/Contract Plan's literal, operational text (the section normally read for the exact shape of a human-copy-staged change) would add a real new CI job, violating AC-016's explicit job-count-unchanged requirement and the binding constraint that the job graph stay byte-unchanged -- an unimplementable, self-contradictory instruction that only a human decision can resolve. Not precheck-owned; distinct evidence from the ARCH-COVERAGE finding (different text: job COUNT vs. `needs:` membership wording).

design.md contains a `## Security Boundaries` section (design.md:481-490) naming four trust boundaries (B1: new-suite fixtures vs. live protected guard binaries; B2: fixture GitHub issue body vs. reading agent session; B3: `test.yml` vs. agent-direct edits; B4: fixture world vs. real repository/network/ledger state), each with an explicit Auth/Authz Mechanism column (e.g. B1: "env-var/PATH indirection... neither suite ever opens either target file for writing"), a Data Classification column (all rows correctly state no PII is present -- "internal repository content only" / "synthetic fixtures only"), and an OWASP Concerns column (Security Misconfiguration, Prompt Injection, Test Isolation). security-spec.md provides a materially deeper layer refinement (STRIDE analysis, Authorization table, Data Classification table, full OWASP Mapping table covering Injection/Prompt Injection/Security Misconfiguration/Broken Access Control/DoS at security-spec.md:79-87), satisfying the cross-layer normative-refinement expectation.

SKIP: design.md contains no `## Frontend Plan` or `## Backend Plan` section (confirmed by direct grep -- zero matches), so the check's scoped surface does not exist. design.md's `Feature Type:` header (design.md:5-8) is a custom descriptive sentence rather than one of the four literal skip-eligible values, but ux-spec.md:1-9 and frontend-spec.md:1-9 are both explicit N/A stubs ("no GUI, view, dialog, menu item, or human interactive shell surface of its own" / "There is no browser or frontend application"), confirming this is functionally a non-UI, CLI/CI-only feature with no frontend surface to check for consistency against the API/Contract Plan.

The `## Test Strategy` section (design.md:362-402) covers unit scope (per-stream fixture-driven suites and what each stubs/mocks, e.g. item 1's decision-parity assertions, item 2's 3x4x3 matrix enumeration discipline), integration scope (item 5: `bash tests/run-all.sh` / `pwsh tests/run-all.ps1` locally, 3-OS CI matrix as authoritative once the human-copy candidate lands), acceptance-criteria verification approach (explicit TEST-001..023 cross-references tied to AC-NNN via the Cross-Layer Dependencies table, design.md:159-168), and concrete planned verification evidence (named suite files, named self-check technique reused from `tests/workflow-state-ci-integration.tests.sh`, named staged-candidate path). Item 7 explicitly and appropriately defers Stream C's test plan pending its ADR-0010 unblock. Given the confirmed non-UI Feature Type (ux-spec.md/frontend-spec.md N/A stubs), no E2E/user-journey path is required; item 5's full-suite + 3-OS-CI-matrix regression is the closest and adequate analog.

Every component referenced in `## Architecture` and `## API / Contract Plan` is defined in the Components table (design.md:94-104) or in a named subsection: `tests/guard-dispatch-fallback.tests.sh`/`tests/guard-negative-corpus.tests.sh`/`sdd-hook-guard.{py,js,ps1,sh}`/`tests/workflow-scenarios/`/`docs/adr/0010-...`/`.github/workflows/test.yml`/`tests/run-all.sh`/`tests/run-all.ps1`/`CHANGELOG.md` all have Components-table rows; the PowerShell forwarding stubs, `guard-cwd-bypass.tests.sh`'s reused corpus, `GUARD_PY`/`GUARD_JS`/`GUARD_PS1`/`GUARD_SH` env-var indirection, and the `plugins/sdd-bootstrap` entry point are each defined inline in their respective API/Contract Plan subsections (design.md:220-243, 247-268, 310-323); `tests/loops/loop-inventory.json` and `required-checks` are established system components per investigation.md INV-014/BL-001 and are further described in design.md prose. The `deterministic-tests-boundary` job (design.md:343-346) is named and described in place (satisfying this check's letter), though its self-contradictory relationship to the rest of the document is a distinct defect already captured under API-COVERAGE (Critical) -- not duplicated here per the calibration doc's evidence-gate item 4.

design.md's `## ADR Change Log` (design.md:170-179) explicitly states "No new ADR. This feature introduces no new vocabulary, schema, or architectural pattern of its own" and lists the verbatim-reuse rationale for each stream. `docs/adr/0010-loop-inventory-and-fixture-vocabulary.md` is referenced only as an existing, read-only dependency (design.md:100, 128-131), not as a decision this design introduces, so the check's file-existence-verification trigger for newly-listed ADRs does not apply; confirmed present on disk regardless (`docs/adr/0010-loop-inventory-and-fixture-vocabulary.md`, existence-only check). design.md's claim that ADR-0010's `Status: Proposed` (design.md:72, 178) is internally consistent with requirements.md's Re-verification note ("ADR-0010's `Status: Proposed(人間承認待ち)`") and investigation.md INV-015 -- no internal inconsistency within the reviewed spec set, so per this review's scope this claim is not flagged (the human-side promotion to Accepted is outside this manifest and outside the internal-consistency test this check applies).

feature_type: Non-UI, CLI/CI-only test-and-CI-hardening feature: three independently shippable new Bash test-suite files plus a protected `.github/workflows/test.yml` job-graph restructuring (human-copy staged), with a fourth stream (workflow-scenario schema) spec-defined but implementation-Blocked pending ADR-0010 approval.
